title: Vue.js Watchers Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of observing data, side effects, and the Watcher vs. Computed distinction.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primary role of a Watcher in Vue.js?
options: To perform side effects (like an API call) when a data property changes.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.
To replace the standard HTML input elements.

questions.question: Which option is used to define Watchers in a Vue instance?
When should you prefer a Watcher over a Computed property?
options: When the logic is synchronous and only returns a simplified value.
When you need to perform an asynchronous operation or any side effect.

questions.question: Does a watcher receive both the new and old value of the property it is watching?
options: Yes, they are passed as the first and second arguments.
No, only the new value is received.

questions.question: Can watchers be used to watch objects or arrays deep (e.g. { deep: true } property)?
options: Yes, using the deep: true option.
No, they only watch basic strings and numbers.
